regions: A narrow strip where wave-smoothed stones meet salt grass and driftwood.
Muddy, root-laced ground sloping down to a river's edge, carved fresh each spring by floodwater.
A long, pale beach exposed at low tide, scattered with shells and the wrack of storms.
A small, sheltered bay tucked between headlands, its calm waters hiding smugglers and secrets.
A yawning arm of the sea reaching deep into the continent, its waters dark and restless.
A wide, protected waterway between mainland and island, busy with sails and shifting currents.
A treacherous narrows where opposing shores funnel wind and tide into a churning gauntlet.
A deep-cut passage of swift water carved between land masses, vital for ships and dangerous in fog.
A slender finger of sea probing inland, its still waters reflecting overhanging cliffs and pines.
